surprise: Fixing the Bergman model equilibrium (clearance on I-Ib, h=Gb) removed artificial homeostatic compensation, making PPO outperform random on ALL three environments -- previously random matched or beat PPO on BasalControl.
mechanism: The original model had dI=-n*I at basal state, causing insulin to drift from equilibrium. With dI=-n*(I-Ib) and h=Gb, the model is truly at equilibrium without exogenous insulin, making random control much less effective and revealing clear RL signal across all environments.
implication: Model equilibrium correctness is critical for RL benchmark validity. Artificial homeostatic compensation can mask the learning signal, making random baselines appear unreasonably strong.
baseline_margin: PPO achieves 921.5 vs random 74.3 on BasalControl, 1101.6 vs -40.7 on BolusAdvisor, 1822.5 vs -3324.3 on ClosedLoop. PPO outperforms on all three environments.
key_finding: After equilibrium fix, PPO consistently outperforms random across all environments with increasing advantage: BasalControl (12.4x), BolusAdvisor (27.1x), ClosedLoop (most dramatic absolute separation). The difficulty gradient is preserved but with much stronger signal.
